response.justification: Wei Chen is an experienced full-stack software developer with 15+ years across web, mobile, and some systems programming, but the profile is fundamentally misaligned with a Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI accelerators. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU or SIMD/MIMD architecture work, deep-learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design. The closest relevant experience — low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby and C/C++ work at National Instruments — is adjacent at best and dated. The candidate meets the years-of-experience bar (BS + 8+ years) but not the domain requirements.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career (15+ years) meeting the BS + 8 years experience requirement
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers) at Dunnhumby
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions and test tooling authorship)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not principal-level hardware-software co-design

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and accelerator architecture experience
Low-latency systems and performance-oriented programming experience
